common.emptyConnectDatabase: Connect to a database from the File menu to browse its schema.
common.languageDescription: Interface language. Date and number formatting still follow your operating system.
common.errorPanelCrashed: Something went wrong rendering this panel.

view.label: View
view.help: Drag panel tabs to rearrange. Drop outside the dock area to float a panel. Counts and sizes are engine estimates.

connectionDialog.description: Credentials are stored in the OS keychain, never written to disk in plaintext.

fields.name: Name
fields.connectionStringHint: Built automatically from the fields above. The password is stored securely and is not embedded.
fields.connectionStringWarning: Editing the connection string by hand can introduce errors — double-check it. Use this for Atlas (mongodb+srv://), replica sets, or extra URI options the form doesn't cover.

ssh.localPortHint: Leave at 0 to auto-assign a free port.
ssh.unavailableForSqlite: SQLite files don't need a tunnel.
ssh.hostKeyPolicyHint: How HuginnDB decides whether to trust the server's SSH public key.
ssh.noTrustedFingerprint: No key trusted yet for this host:port.
ssh.forgetConfirm: Forget the saved SSH host key for {{hostPort}}?
ssh.forgetDone: Host key forgotten. The next connection will trust whatever the server presents (when the policy is "Trust on first use").
ssh.forgetNone: No host key was stored for this host.
ssh.unavailableForSrv: SSH tunnelling is not available for mongodb+srv:// connections (SRV resolves to multiple hosts). Use a direct mongodb://host:port URI to tunnel.

connectionDialog.managerDescription: Pick a connection on the left to edit it, or create a new one.

connectPassword.title: Password required
connectPassword.description: The stored password for "{{name}}" could not be found. Enter it to connect.

adhocDriver.title: Which database driver?
adhocDriver.description: "{{name}}" was launched without --driver and no default driver is set. Pick the backend to connect with.
adhocDriver.note: Tip: set a default driver in Settings → General to skip this next time.

cliConnect.title: Open incoming connection
cliConnect.description: A new launch wants to open "{{name}}". Open it in this window, or in a new one?

feedback.title: Report or suggest
feedback.description: File a bug or a feature request to the project's GitHub tracker.
feedback.descPlaceholder: What happened, what you expected, steps to reproduce…
feedback.hintApi: The issue will be created directly on GitHub.
feedback.hintBrowser: Without a token, a pre-filled GitHub page opens for you to submit.
feedback.openedBrowser: Opened a pre-filled issue in your browser
feedback.noGithub: I don't have a GitHub account
feedback.openedMailClient: Opened a pre-filled email in your mail app

drop.title: Drop table {{name}}?
drop.description: This permanently removes the table and all of its data. This cannot be undone.

createDatabase.title: New database
createDatabase.description: Create a new database on this server.
createDatabase.createdSingleDb: Database "{{name}}" created. This connection is scoped to a single database — open a new connection (or a multi-database one) to browse into it.

security.title: Security
security.empty: This driver has no server-side user/permission model.

cellEditor.title: Cell editor
cellEditor.sideEmpty: Right-click a cell and choose "Open in side editor" to edit it here.
cellEditor.discardChangesConfirm: You have unsaved changes in the side editor. Discard them and switch to the clicked cell?

structure.rebuildBody: SQLite can't alter this in place, so the table will be recreated: a new table is built, the data is copied over, the old table is dropped and the new one renamed. Make sure you have a backup if the data matters.

tabs.emptyOpenSomething: Open a table from the schema explorer, or create a new query tab.
tabs.emptyConnectFirst: Select or create a connection to begin.

settings.title: Preferences
settings.description: Changes apply live. The preferences file lives in your platform's config directory; the dialog is also available with

defaultDriver.label: Default database driver
defaultDriver.desc: Driver used when a connection is created without picking one — CLI launches without --driver, and the New connection form. "Ask each time" prompts on CLI launches instead of guessing.

confirmDestructive.label: Confirm destructive actions
confirmDestructive.desc: Ask for confirmation before deleting rows, dropping tables, or other irreversible operations.

restoreTabs.label: Restore tabs when opening a connection
restoreTabs.desc: Reopen the SQL and data tabs you had open the last time you used this connection.

queryHistoryLimit.label: Query history size
queryHistoryLimit.desc: Maximum number of recent queries kept in the history panel.

cellEditorMode.label: Cell editor
cellEditorMode.desc: Where a large cell value opens when you expand it: a centered dialog or a docked right-side panel.

wordWrap.label: Soft-wrap long lines
wordWrap.desc: Wrap lines that exceed the viewport instead of horizontal scrolling.

minimap.label: Show minimap
minimap.desc: Tiny overview on the right edge of the editor.

formatOnPaste.label: Format on paste
formatOnPaste.desc: Apply Monaco's basic SQL formatter to pasted snippets.

nullDisplay.label: NULL display
nullDisplay.desc: String used to render SQL NULL values in the grid.

truncateLongTextAt.label: Truncate text at (chars)
truncateLongTextAt.desc: Cap how many characters a cell renders in the grid; the full value stays available in the preview/editor. 0 disables.

zebraStripes.label: Zebra stripes
zebraStripes.desc: Alternate row background for easier scanning.

stickyHeader.label: Sticky header
stickyHeader.desc: Pin column headers while scrolling vertically.

cellPreview.label: Cell preview panel
cellPreview.desc: Show the floating value preview when a cell is selected. Off keeps single-click as pure navigation.

schemaMetric.label: Schema tree metric
schemaMetric.desc: Number rendered next to each table name in the schema explorer.

bitDisplay.label: BIT display
bitDisplay.desc: How MySQL BIT columns are rendered in the grid.

appearance.builtinInfo: Built-in theme — edits will fork into a new custom theme.

shortcuts.roadmap: Customising shortcuts is on the roadmap. The bindings below are the current built-in defaults.
shortcuts.commitSearch: Commit search / filter in the data grid
shortcuts.closeDialog: Close the active dialog or popover

about.tagline: Minimal desktop database manager for PostgreSQL, MySQL and SQLite.
about.tabStateHint: A sibling {{file}} stores the open tabs and schema-tree expansion for each connection.

export.title: Export profiles
export.securityWarning: Passwords are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. Keep your passphrase safe — it cannot be recovered.

import.title: Import profiles
import.pickDescription: Select a HuginnDB profile export file (.json) to import.
import.passphraseRequired: This file contains encrypted passwords. Enter the passphrase used when it was exported.
import.conflictsDescription: {{count}} profile(s) share an ID with an existing one. Choose how to handle each:
import.needsPassword: {{count}} profile(s) were imported without a password. Open each connection to set a password before connecting.

console.emptyNoActivity: No backend activity yet. Run a query or open a connection.
console.emptyNoMatch: No entries match the current filter.

query.runAllTitle: Run every statement in order on one connection
query.noResults: Run a query to see results.

saved.title: Saved Queries
saved.emptyNone: No saved queries yet. Use the bookmark icon in a query tab to save the current SQL.

saveQuery.description: Saved locally to your machine. Available across all connections.
saveQuery.name: Name

update.toastDescription: Download and install it from inside the app — it will relaunch automatically when done.
